title: Give up (renounce) Canadian citizenship: Who can apply - Canada.ca
content:
## Give up (renounce) Canadian citizenship: Who can apply
- 1. About the process
The eligibility requirements to renounce your Canadian citizenship depend on your situation.
Generally , if you’re a Canadian citizen who wants to give up your citizenship, you
	must meet all of the requirements below :
- be a citizen of a country other than Canada or become a citizen of a country other than Canada if your
		application to renounce is approved
- be at least 18 years old
- not be a threat to Canada’s security or be involved in ongoing criminal activity
- not be prevented from understanding the significance of giving up your Canadian citizenship because of a mental
		disability
- not be subject to revocation of citizenship proceedings

### If you automatically got citizenship when the Citizenship Act changed
There are fewer requirements if you’re giving up your Canadian citizenship because you automatically became a
		citizen when changes
		were made to the Citizenship Act . In this situation, you must
- be a citizen of a country other than Canada or become a citizen of a country other than Canada if your application to give up your citizenship is approved
- not be prevented from understanding the significance of giving up your Canadian citizenship because of a mental disability

### Give feedback about this page
Thank you for your feedback.
